# Kestrel Institute Annual Report

## Research Portfolio

The Kestrel Institute administered four active programmes this year. Project Meridian led by headcount and budget, followed in size by the Basalt Composites durability study, the Coastal Tidal Array pilot, and an internal metrology fellowship.

## Funding Breakdown

Funding reached the Kestrel Institute from three streams. A national research council grant supplied the largest share and underpinned Project Meridian entirely. A private endowment funds the metrology fellowship and Basalt Composites overhead. Licensing royalties from the Halden Reactor drain patent — assigned to the Kestrel Institute under Dr. Elara Voss's employment agreement — covered the Coastal Tidal Array pilot and contributed a surplus to reserves.

## Personnel

Dr. Elara Voss served as the most senior programme director for the third consecutive year. The Basalt Composites and Coastal Tidal Array studies were each led by junior investigators reporting to the same directorate. The metrology fellow operates independently but uses Kestrel Institute laboratory space under a facilities agreement.

## Outlook

The Kestrel Institute anticipates a transition in year six: Project Meridian moves from development to field-trial phase, Basalt Composites closes, and a new atmospheric-chemistry programme is expected to open pending grant confirmation.
